name: Barranco do Velho — circular route (PR17)
region_intro: Hiking around Salir, Portugal, offers diverse terrain characterized by rolling hills, valleys, and the unique geological feature of the Dune of Salir. The region features a mix of coastal landscapes and inland trails, often traversing Mediterranean scrubland and rocky outcrops. Protected areas around Salir provide opportunities to explore varied ecosystems and natural features. Best hiking trails around Salir The most popular hiking route is Barranco do Velho — circular route (PR17), a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete, offering an accessible loop through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pé do Coelho Trail (PR6 LLE), a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path that explores the rural surroundings of Salir. Local hikers also love the View south from Rocha da Pena – Rocha da Pena loop from Paisagem Protegida Local da Rocha da Pena, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through the prominent Rocha da Pena, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Salir is defined by its varied hills, valleys, and the distinctive Rocha da Pena, offering a mix of coastal and inland experiences. The network provides options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging routes. The routes in Salir are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,400 reviews. More than 5,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Salir's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Salir?
A: Salir offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with nearly 40 distinct trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different preferences, including easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging long-distance hikes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Salir?
A: The terrain around Salir is quite varied, characterized by rolling hills, valleys, and distinctive geological features. You'll often traverse Mediterranean scrubland and rocky outcrops. The region offers a mix of coastal landscapes and inland trails, providing diverse scenery for hikers.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in Salir?
A: Yes, Salir has several easy routes suitable for families. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421609900/barranco-do-velho-circular-route-pr17" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Velho — circular route (PR17)&lt;/a&gt;. This 2.8-mile (4.5 km) trail is classified as easy and offers an accessible loop through the local landscape, perfect for a leisurely outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Salir?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Salir are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421609900/barranco-do-velho-circular-route-pr17" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Velho — circular route (PR17)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy option, and for a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5286048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View south from Rocha da Pena – Rocha da Pena loop from Paisagem Protegida Local da Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance hikes in the Salir area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32183138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salir Parish Church 🇵🇹 – Eira de Agosto loop from Salir&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.0 km (16.8 miles) route. This trail features significant elevation changes, offering a strenuous but rewarding experience through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking near Salir?
A: Salir is known for its unique natural features. You can explore the prominent Rocha da Pena, which offers stunning viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/219453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trig Point and Panoramic View at Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the distinctive Dune of Salir, a significant landform composed of wind-driven sand. Additionally, you might encounter natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Benémola Stream&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/460116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrocal Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit on hikes around Salir?
A: Yes, you can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/460116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrocal Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is listed as a natural monument in the area. While specific hiking routes directly to it are not detailed here, it's a notable natural attraction worth seeking out in the region.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Salir?
A: The hiking routes in Salir are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 1,400 reviews. More than 5,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Salir's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Is there public transport access to hiking trailheads in Salir?
A: While Salir is a rural area, local public transport options may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider using a car to reach specific trailheads, especially for routes further outside the main village. Parking information is often available on individual route pages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Salir?
A: Salir, being in Portugal, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and blooming flora. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is also possible, with cooler temperatures but generally less rainfall than other European regions.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the trails?
A: Many trails around Salir offer excellent viewpoints, particularly those that ascend the region's hills and peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5286048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View south from Rocha da Pena – Rocha da Pena loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its scenic vistas. You can also find panoramic views from highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/219453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trig Point and Panoramic View at Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Salir suitable for dogs?
A: Many natural areas and trails in Portugal are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, especially around livestock or wildlife. It's always best to check specific local regulations or signs at trailheads. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog, especially on longer routes or during warmer months, as natural water sources may be scarce.
tips: Barranco do Velho is a mountain village in the south of the Serra do Caldeirão in the Portuguese Algarve in the municipality of Loulé. The village was merged with the freguesia Salir in the administrative reorganization of 2013. The village is located on the N2, which used to be the main road from Faro to Lisbon. The landscape is dominated by cork oaks, holm oaks and strawberry trees. For many Portuguese it is a popular hunting ground. The main activity of the people in the region is cork extraction. There is a nature trail in the village with the theme of biodiversity. There are also several hiking trails through the village, for example the 300-kilometre long-distance hiking trail (GR trail) Via Algarviana (GR13). The church on a hill above the village is relatively new. It was built in 1944, financed by two brothers from Germany who both owned a house there.

The Casa dos Cantoneiros in Barranco do Velho, on Portugal's emblematic EN2, is being transformed into a tourist attraction. The revitalization project, part of a broader EN2 revitalization program, will preserve local history and culture while offering a unique experience to visitors. The restoration of Casa dos Cantoneiros combines respect for the historical heritage with the offer of a modern and attractive space for tourists.

Tia Bia is a restaurant and local accommodation located in Barranco do Velho, at the foot of Serra do Caldeirão, an area of rare scenic beauty, with ancient fountains and 5 km from the international motocross circuit of Cortelha. Area with many wild boars and other species.
A pleasant refuge in the mountains, 30 km from the beaches of Vilamoura and Quarteira, the island of Faro and Albufeira. 30 km from Faro International Airport.
50 km from the border of Vila Real de Santo António/Ayamonte.
Barranco do Velho is a formidable crossroads in the middle of the Serra do Caldeirão.
